prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for furan (9 atoms: O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) using the fukui-reactivity chemkit skill.

Method provenance:
- Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f+, f-, f0, dual descriptor) via finite-difference Mulliken charges (PySCF)
- Level of theory: DFT, tier 'standard' — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, exact four-centre integrals; density fitting OFF)
- Geometry provenance: User-supplied .xyz at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz; single-point (geometry not re-optimized)
- Solvent/environment: Gas phase (no solvent)
- Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (neutral singlet); cation: +1 / doublet; anion: −1 / doublet
- Software: PySCF (chemkit DFT backend)
- Convergence: All three SCF calculations (neutral, cation, anion) converged; integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true
- Warnings: None reported; no warnings array entries.
- Integrity checks: sum(f+) = 1.0000 ✅, sum(f−) = 1.0000 ✅, schema header ✅

Headline result: The most electrophilic site is atom 5 (C), f+ = 1.9242931. The most nucleophilic site is also atom 5 (C), f− = 0.16164. The dual descriptor peaks at atom 5 (C), Δf = +1.76266, indicating dominant electrophilic character at the α-carbons of the furan ring.

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output JSON: furan_fukui_dft.json
Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260704-053741_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
